70633fb1729858b85d700f856425889a145bc880.title: Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling
70633fb1729858b85d700f856425889a145bc880.description:
The Law Centre today delivered a formal letter to Dr. Jerome Larkin, Director of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH), demanding that the agency immediately cease enforcement of Rhode Island’s ban on conversion therapy for minors. The action follows the U.S. Supreme Court’s March 21, 2026 decision in Chiles v. Salazar, which struck down Colorado’s similar law as a violation of the First Amendment.

The post Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

9b31969d1b902df8f3a559e5da0ba6b586b10920.title: Why Iowa?
9b31969d1b902df8f3a559e5da0ba6b586b10920.description:
From a public policy standpoint, Iowa has become a national leader in conservative reform. On taxes, budgeting, regulation, school choice, and protections for life and civil liberties, the state stands in sharp contrast to neighbors such as Minnesota and Illinois. But policy alone does not fully explain why so many people love Iowa. “What happened this year went beyond Iowa nice. You showed a humanity that rivals what most of us have seen in our lifetime,” stated Gov. Kim Reynolds in her 2021 Condition of the State Address. Reynolds was referencing the numerous humanitarian acts in response to the COVID pandemic and the devastating storms. Across Iowa, individuals, organizations and businesses stepped up to help one another and their communities during these crises.

The post Why Iowa? appeared first on ITR Foundation.

231a4983daaf4b734e44b3c323e5fea872eafe90.title: Law Centre Calls on Rhode Island Department of Health to Stop Enforcing Conversion Therapy Ban After Supreme Court Ruling
231a4983daaf4b734e44b3c323e5fea872eafe90.description:
The Law Centre today delivered a formal letter to Dr. Jerome Larkin, Director of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH), demanding that the agency immediately cease enforcement of Rhode Island’s ban on conversion therapy for minors. The action follows the U.S. Supreme Court’s March 21, 2026 decision in Chiles v. Salazar, which struck down Colorado’s similar law as a violation of the First Amendment.
